unit.note: server vitest 161 files / 1887 tests green (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local). The three new frozen guard files (author!=fixer, must-not-modify) contribute 16 of these: sdk-sessions-store-merge.test.ts (6), sdk-sessions-store-merge-edge.test.ts (6), sdk-sessions-store-concurrency.test.ts (4). OS-deterministic - the same guards run on ubuntu CI.

integration.note: The 16 frozen guards drive the REAL SdkSessionsStore.persist() merge-under-lock path with real proper-lockfile, real fs, and an isolated temp registryDir (the rename/lock seams injected only to simulate mid-write failure + win32 EBUSY). Every guard was RED-first on pre-fix main and GREEN after the fix. Author != fixer; the fix agent could not weaken them.

e2e.note: No E2E for D04. This is a cross-instance shared-file data-integrity invariant on the server (two OS processes racing one ~/.shipwright-webui/sdk-sessions.json); no browser can drive it and there is no UI-observable change. The deterministic server guards are the behavioral proof (surface=none, justified below).

surface_verification.justification: Server-side sdk-sessions.json persistence: persist() now re-reads + 3-way merges under the proper-lockfile critical section and writes atomically (tmp+rename) — restoring the intended no-lost-update + claim-field-preservation guarantee when two documented concurrent webui instances (autostart-prod + PORT-override dev; parallel worktrees) share ~/.shipwright-webui/sdk-sessions.json (FR-01.08/FR-01.09). NO UI-observable change: the task store create/patch/delete contract is byte-identical; this is a data-integrity invariant, not a visible feature. Behavioral proof is 16 frozen guards (author!=fixer, RED-first on pre-fix main) over the REAL SdkSessionsStore with real proper-lockfile + real fs on an isolated temp registryDir: sdk-sessions-store-merge.test.ts (foreign-row/claim-field survival, 3-way field merge, atomic write), sdk-sessions-store-merge-edge.test.ts (delete-resurrection, unreadable/future-schema disk preservation), sdk-sessions-store-concurrency.test.ts (swap-after-write lost-update, transient-read reject). No browser can drive two OS processes racing one shared JSON file, so there is no post-merge UI smoke. Full server suite 1887 green (161 files, SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local); OS-deterministic on ubuntu CI. Zero client/** files changed (the frontend-change detector false-positives on the 'src/' substring inside 'server/src/').

behaviors.behavior: AC1/Guard1 - a foreign task row written out-of-band to sdk-sessions.json survives an instance-A persist() of an unrelated patch (no whole-file clobber)
behaviors.evidence: sdk-sessions-store-merge.test.ts - RED on pre-fix main (persist rewrote the whole file from the boot cache, dropping the foreign row); GREEN after the merge-under-lock fix. Real SdkSessionsStore, real proper-lockfile, real fs on an isolated temp registryDir.

behaviors.behavior: AC1/Guard1 - externally-written claim fields (claimToken/claimedBy) survive A's persist AND become visible in A's in-memory view so the 409 claim guard can fire
behaviors.evidence: sdk-sessions-store-merge.test.ts - RED on pre-fix (claim clobbered + invisible); GREEN: the 3-way merge refreshes the in-memory map from the merged result.

behaviors.behavior: AC1 - field-level 3-way merge on a row both sides touched: baseline->memory diff applied over the disk row, same-field last-writer-wins; A's own mutation is present
behaviors.evidence: sdk-sessions-store-merge.test.ts - asserts A's patched field wins on the field A changed while a concurrently-written disk field on the same row is retained.

behaviors.behavior: AC2/Guard2 - two store instances over one shared file with N interleaved persist() calls: the union of all rows survives (no lost update)
behaviors.evidence: sdk-sessions-store-merge.test.ts - interleave scenario; RED on pre-fix main (last-writer-wins erased the other instance's rows).

behaviors.behavior: Guard2 - load->persist->load round-trip equality and no unintended schemaVersion bump (stays v4)
behaviors.evidence: sdk-sessions-store-merge.test.ts - round-trip equality + schemaVersion invariant assertions.

behaviors.behavior: Guard3 - atomicity: a mid-write interruption never leaves sdk-sessions.json truncated (tmp+rename, injected rename seam)
behaviors.evidence: sdk-sessions-store-merge.test.ts - simulated mid-write failure leaves the prior complete file intact.

behaviors.behavior: Edge - a row this instance deleted since baseline is NOT resurrected by concurrent disk churn (deletedSinceBaseline reconciliation within the persist transaction)
behaviors.evidence: sdk-sessions-store-merge-edge.test.ts - RED for same-instance delete-resurrection; GREEN after deletedSinceBaseline reconciliation.

behaviors.behavior: Edge - A's own rows are preserved when the on-disk file is unreadable/corrupt at re-read time (classifyDiskRaw parse-status fallback), never self-dropped
behaviors.evidence: sdk-sessions-store-merge-edge.test.ts - RED for own-row drop on unreadable disk; GREEN via classifyDiskRaw.

behaviors.behavior: Edge - a disk file tagged a FUTURE schemaVersion is preserved (no downgrade clobber); persist aborts rather than overwriting a newer-binary write
behaviors.evidence: sdk-sessions-store-merge-edge.test.ts + sdk-sessions-store-concurrency.test.ts - future-schema disk preserved; intentional fail-loud abort.

behaviors.behavior: Concurrency - swap-after-write lost-update (a regression the remediation itself introduced) is fixed: keep the live map, snapshot pre-write, baseline=written-bytes, reconcile only pre-write deletes
behaviors.evidence: sdk-sessions-store-concurrency.test.ts - RED reproduced the swap-after-write discard of a concurrent mutation; GREEN after the live-map-preserving rewrite.

behaviors.behavior: Concurrency - a transient I/O error re-reading under the lock retries (rule-6 budget) then REJECTS, never clobbers disk with a partial/empty view
Concurrency/Edge - Windows rename EBUSY/EPERM/EACCES on the atomic rename is retried before surfacing (win32 rename flakiness)
behaviors.evidence: sdk-sessions-store-concurrency.test.ts - rename-retry guard exercises the withFsRetry wrapper on the injected rename seam.

test_completeness.note: AC1-AC5 (5) all covered. AC3 (full suites green) is the 1887-test suite; AC4 (footprint/anti-ratchet) and AC5 (MAX gates: independent RED-first guards + doubt-reviewer x3 + external openrouter x3) are process gates recorded in the decision-drop, not per-behavior rows. Every enumerated behavior is tested; untested_testable=0.
